'''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft}} (comprehensive park) located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushiro Futago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=draft}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=draft}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=draft}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushiro Futago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道|12=draft}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|ja|伊勢崎駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|ja|両毛線|12=draft}}.
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Mae Futago Kofun, Naka Futago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Omuro Park}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/gyomu/3/3548.html Maebashi City Ōmuro Park] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.maebashi-cvb.com/k_spot/park/omuro.htm |title=Maebashi Comprehensive Guide: Ōmuro Park |date=20151101082103}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=Gunma Prefecture}}

<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushiro Futago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>

== External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/gyomu/3/3548.html Maebashi City Ōmuro Park] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.maebashi-cvb.com/k_spot/park/omuro.htm |title=Maebashi Comprehensive Guide: Ōmuro Park |date=20151101082103}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=Gunma Prefecture}} <!--Omuro Park-->
{{draft categories| [[Category:Parks in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Maebashi]] [[Category:Japanese Folk House Gardens]] [[Category:Omuro Park]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Omuro Park]] [[ja:大室公園]] [[en:Omuro Park]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omuro Park}}

'''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft}} (comprehensive park) located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushiro Futago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=draft}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=draft}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=draft}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushiro Futago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道|12=draft}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|ja|伊勢崎駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|ja|両毛線|12=draft}}.
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Mae Futago Kofun, Naka Futago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Omuro Park}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/gyomu/3/3548.html Maebashi City Ōmuro Park] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.maebashi-cvb.com/k_spot/park/omuro.htm |title=Maebashi Comprehensive Guide: Ōmuro Park |date=20151101082103}}

revision.text:
{{公園 |名称 = 大室公園 |英語名称 = Omuro Park |画像 = [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|300px|時の広場]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=14}} |画像キャプション = 時の広場 |国 = {{JPN}} |都市 = [[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 | 緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 14.9 | 経度度 = 139|経度分 = 12|経度秒 = 1.9 |分類 = [[都市公園]]（総合公園） |面積 = 37ha |前身 = [[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] |開園 = |運営者 = 前橋市 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |駐車場 = |バリアフリー = |建築家と技術者 = |告示 = |事務所 = |事務所所在地 = |アクセス = |備考 = |公式サイト = [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html 前橋市のサイト] }} [[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|大室公園整備前の大室古墳群
{{国土航空写真}}（1986年度撮影）。]] '''大室公園'''（おおむろこうえん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]に位置する[[都市公園]]（総合公園）である。[[西大室町]]と[[東大室町]]にまたがる[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html 都市公園法による公園の名称及び所在地について]（前橋市告示）。[[日本の歴史公園100選]]に選ばれている。 == 来歴 == 赤城山麓の標高130メートルの台地に築造された[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]は、日本の古墳のなかでも早い時期から学術的な調査が行われたことで有名である。[[1878年]]（明治11年）には豊城入彦命の陵墓候補地に挙げられていた[[前二子古墳]]の発掘調査が行われ、石室より発見された数多くの遺物は、当時の世間の注目を集めた。これらの古墳群を「大室公園」として整備する計画が[[昭和]]60年代から県の公園部局と文化財部局の主導の元進められた。実際の整備は、1989年（平成元年）に大室公園史跡整備委員会が組織され、1991年（平成3年）から発掘調査が始まった。まず1991年（平成3年）に[[後二子古墳]]と[[小二子古墳]]、1992年（平成4年）に前二子古墳、1993年（平成5年）と1994年（平成6年）に[[中二子古墳]]、1995年（平成7年）と1996年（平成8年）に小二子古墳と6年間にわたって調査された。南北500m、東西1000mの広がりを持ち、公園内には国史跡の大室古墳群や豪族居館跡といった遺跡のほか、江戸時代末期の[[赤城型民家]]や古墳時代の住居が再現されている。 == 園内 == * [[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] * [[梅木遺跡]]（豪族居館跡） * 民家園 - 旧関根家住宅（前橋市指定重要文化財）、古代住居 File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[前二子古墳]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[中二子古墳]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[後二子古墳]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[小二子古墳]] File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|旧関根家住宅 File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|古代住居 File:Goryo-numa.jpg|五料沼 File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|大室公園のコナラ（前橋市指定天然記念物） == 交通アクセス == * [[北関東自動車道]]波志江スマートインターチェンジから3.6km。（車で10分） * [[北関東自動車道]]伊勢崎インターチェンジから5km。(車で10分) * [[上毛電気鉄道]]大胡駅からタクシーで10分。 * [[両毛線]]伊勢崎駅からタクシーで20分。 == 注釈・出典 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *前原 豊「東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕」　シリーズ・遺跡を学ぶ 063　新泉社　2010年 *前橋市教育委員会(編) 「前橋市 大室古墳群-前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」 2013年 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Omuro Park}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/gyomu/3/3548.html 前橋市 大室公園] * {{Wayback |url=http://www.maebashi-cvb.com/k_spot/park/omuro.htm |title=前橋まるごとガイド 大室公園 |date=20151101082103}} {{Pref-stub|pref=群馬県}} {{デフォルトソート:おおむろこうえん}} [[Category:群馬県の公園]] [[Category:前橋市の地理]] [[Category:日本の民家園]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17227958]] Wikidata name: Omuro Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Mae Futago Kofun]], believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Mae Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|qq=|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|qq=|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Mae Futago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|qq=|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|qq=|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].
== Notes and References ==
<references/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17227958]] Wikidata name: Omuro Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Mae Futago Kofun]], believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Mae Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|qq=|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|qq=|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Mae Futago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|qq=|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|qq=|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].

<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Mae Futago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Mae Futago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Mae Futago Kofun" to "Maefutago Kofun"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17227958]] Wikidata name: Omuro Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.

The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.

== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Omuro Park}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/gyomu/3/3548.html Maebashi City Ōmuro Park] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.maebashi-cvb.com/k_spot/park/omuro.htm |title=Maebashi Comprehensive Guide: Ōmuro Park |date=20151101082103}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17227958]] Wikidata name: Omuro Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Omuro Park}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/gyomu/3/3548.html Maebashi City Ōmuro Park] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.maebashi-cvb.com/k_spot/park/omuro.htm |title=Maebashi Comprehensive Guide: Ōmuro Park |date=20151101082103}}

== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.

<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17227958]] Wikidata name: Omuro Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.

== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17227958]] Wikidata name: Omuro Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushiro Futago Kofun]] File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kita-Kantō Expressway]] (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Isesaki Station|Isesaki Station]] on the [[:en:Ryōmō Line|Ryōmō Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17227958]] Wikidata name: Omuro Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}}, believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}}, {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}}, and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|en|Isesaki Station|WD=Q5963232|lt=Isesaki Station}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line}}.
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Omuro Park}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/gyomu/3/3548.html Maebashi City Ōmuro Park] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.maebashi-cvb.com/k_spot/park/omuro.htm |title=Maebashi Comprehensive Guide: Ōmuro Park |date=20151101082103}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=Gunma Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|大室公園|version=99440359|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}}, believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}}, {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}}, and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. == Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>

<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.

Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}}, {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}}, and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|en|Isesaki Station|WD=Q5963232|lt=Isesaki Station}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line}}.
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Omuro Park}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/gyomu/3/3548.html Maebashi City Ōmuro Park] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.maebashi-cvb.com/k_spot/park/omuro.htm |title=Maebashi Comprehensive Guide: Ōmuro Park |date=20151101082103}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]]
'''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}}, believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}}, {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}}, and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Akagigata folk houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|en|Isesaki Station|WD=Q5963232|lt=Isesaki Station}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line}}.
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.

<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun|1=Maefutago Kofun}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|1=Nakafutago Kofun}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|1=Ushiro Futago Kofun}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kita-Kantō Expressway|1=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station|1=Ōgo Station}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|en|Isesaki Station|WD=Q5963232|lt=Isesaki Station|1=Isesaki Station}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Omuro Park]][[ja:大室公園]]
'''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun|1=Maefutago Kofun}}, believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun|1=Maefutago Kofun}}, {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|1=Ushiro Futago Kofun}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|1=Nakafutago Kofun}}, and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Q122915237|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun|1=Maefutago Kofun}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|1=Nakafutago Kofun}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|1=Ushiro Futago Kofun}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kita-Kantō Expressway|1=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station|1=Ōgo Station}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|en|Isesaki Station|WD=Q5963232|lt=Isesaki Station|1=Isesaki Station}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}.
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
