== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun|1=Maefutago Kofun}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|1=Nakafutago Kofun}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|1=Ushiro Futago Kofun}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kita-Kantō Expressway|1=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station|1=Ōgo Station}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|en|Isesaki Station|WD=Q5963232|lt=Isesaki Station|1=Isesaki Station}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}.
== Notes and References ==
<references/>
== Bibliography == * Toyo Maehara, "The Power of Eastern Great Clans: Ōmuro Kofun Group," ''Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites,'' Volume 63, Shinseisha, 2010. * Maebashi City Board of Education (ed.), ''Maebashi City Ōmuro Kofun Group: Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushiro Futago Kofun, Sho Futago Kofun,'' 2013.

revision.text:
'''Ōmuro Park''' (大室公園) is an {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (comprehensive park) located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. The park spans the districts of Nishi-Ōmuro and Higashi-Ōmuro and is listed among the {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}<ref>[https://www1.g-reiki.net/maebashi/reiki_honbun/e202RG00000617.html Names and Locations of Parks under the Urban Parks Act] (Maebashi City Notice).</ref>.
== History == Ōmuro Park is situated on a plateau at an elevation of 130 meters on the southern slope of Mount Akagi<ref name="ze" />. The area is notable for the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}}—a cluster of kofun (ancient Kofuns)—that underwent some of the earliest academic archaeological investigations in Japan. In 1878, the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun|1=Maefutago Kofun}}, believed to be the burial site of Toyokuninomikoto, was excavated. Artifacts discovered in the stone chambers attracted widespread attention at the time.
Plans to develop the area as Ōmuro Park began in the late Shōwa period, led by Gunma Prefecture's parks and cultural heritage departments. In 1989, the Ōmuro Park Historic Site Development Committee was formed, and archaeological surveys commenced in 1991. Over the next six years, multiple mounds were investigated, including the {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun|1=Maefutago Kofun}}, {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|1=Ushiro Futago Kofun}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|1=Nakafutago Kofun}}, and {{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}}. The park now encompasses a 500m x 1000m area, featuring national historic sites, remnants of ancient mansions, Edo-period {{ill|Akagigata folk houses|ja|赤城型民家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagigata folk houses|1=Q122915237|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122915237}}, and reconstructed dwellings from the Kofun period.
== Features == The park includes: * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Ruins|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Ruins|1=Umeki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217678}} (remains of a clan mansion) * Folk House Garden - featuring the {{ill|Former Sekine Family Residence|ja|旧関根家住宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sekine Family Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a Maebashi City-designated Important Cultural Property) and ancient dwellings.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun|1=Maefutago Kofun}} File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|1=Nakafutago Kofun}} File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|1=Ushiro Futago Kofun}} File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|{{ill|Sho Futago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} File:Omuro Park Minka-en 2.jpg|Former Sekine Family Residence File:Omuro Park ancient houses.jpg|Ancient dwellings File:Goryo-numa.jpg|Goryō Marsh File:Konara in Omuro Park.jpg|Konara oak in Ōmuro Park (designated as a Natural Monument by Maebashi City) </gallery>
== Access == * 3.6 km from the {{ill|Hashie Smart Interchange|ja|波志江スマートインターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashie Smart Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 波志江パーキングエリア|1=Hashie parking area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555537}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kita-Kantō Expressway|1=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} (10 minutes by car). * 5 km from the {{ill|Isesaki Interchange|ja|伊勢崎インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Interchange|1=Isesaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884861}} on the Kita-Kantō Expressway (10 minutes by car). * 10 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station|1=Ōgo Station}} on the {{ill|Jōmō Electric Railway|ja|上毛電気鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmō Electric Railway|1=Jomo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359477}}. * 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Isesaki Station|en|Isesaki Station|WD=Q5963232|lt=Isesaki Station|1=Isesaki Station}} on the {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}.
